pull_requests.title: Revamp MedicalCodingAgent Architecture and Enhanced External Integrations
pull_requests.body:
## This pull request introduces significant architectural and functionality updates to the `MedicalCodingAgent` project. Below is a high-level summary of the changes:

### Major Changes:
1. **Modularization and Interface Implementation:**
   - Introduced a new `LLMInterface` abstract class to decouple the logic for different LLM backend integrations.
   - Added specific implementations for OpenAI and Mistral backends (`OpenAIImplementation`, `MistralImplementation`).

2. **Enhanced PDF Generation:**
   - Created a `PDFBuilder` class to handle the generation of PDFs with better modularity and dynamic layout support.

3. **CLI Integration for LLM Selection:**
   - Added a command-line interface (`main.py`) to allow users to select between OpenAI and Mistral for LLM processing.

4. **Improved Dependency and Environment Setup:**
   - Updated `requirements.txt` to reflect new dependencies.
   - Added a `setup.sh` script for streamlined environment setup, including system depe

pull_requests.title: Replace Ant Design with MUIX
pull_requests.body:
Replace Ant Design components with MUIX components across the project.

* **Dependencies**:
  - Remove `antd` and `@ant-design/nextjs-registry` from `frontend/package.json`.
  - Add `@mui/material`, `@emotion/react`, `@emotion/styled`, and `@mui/icons-material` to `frontend/package.json`.

* **Sign-In Page**:
  - Replace Ant Design `Form`, `Input`, and `Button` with MUIX `TextField`, `Button`, and `Box` in `frontend/src/app/(auth)/sign-in/page.tsx`.

* **Layouts**:
  - Replace Ant Design `Layout` with MUIX `Box` and `Grid` in `frontend/src/app/(protected)/chat/layout.tsx`, `frontend/src/app/(protected)/dashboard/layout.tsx`, and `frontend/src/app/(protected)/studio/layout.tsx`.

* **Chat Page**:
  - Replace Ant Design `List`, `Input`, `Button`, and `Typography` with MUIX equivalents in `frontend/src/app/(protected)/chat/page.tsx`.

* **Dashboard Page**:
  - Replace Ant Design `Typography`, `Row`, `Col`, and `Card` with MUIX equivalents in `frontend/src/app/(protected)/dashboard/page.ts

pull_requests.title: Bump pnpm from 10.22.0 to 10.28.2
pull_requests.body:
Bumps [pnpm](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/tree/HEAD/pnpm) from 10.22.0 to 10.28.2.
Release notes

Sourced from pnpm's releases.

pnpm 10.28.2

Patch Changes

  • Security fix: prevent path traversal in directories.bin field.

  • When pnpm installs a file: or git: dependency, it now validates that symlinks point within the package directory. Symlinks to paths outside the package root are skipped to prevent local data from being leaked into node_modules.

    This fixes a security issue where a malicious package could create symlinks to sensitive files (e.g., /etc/passwd, ~/.ssh/id_rsa) and have their contents copied when the package is installed.

    Note: This only affects file: and git: dependencies. Registry packages (npm) have syml

pull_requests.title: Bump pnpm from 10.22.0 to 10.27.0
pull_requests.body:
Bumps [pnpm](https://github.com/pnpm/pnpm/tree/HEAD/pnpm) from 10.22.0 to 10.27.0.
Release notes

Sourced from pnpm's releases.

pnpm 10.27

Minor Changes

  • Adding trustPolicyIgnoreAfter allows you to ignore trust policy checks for packages published more than a specified time ago#10352.

  • Added project registry for global virtual store prune support.

    Projects using the store are now registered via symlinks in {storeDir}/v10/projects/. This enables pnpm store prune to track which packages are still in use by active projects and safely remove unused packages from the global virtual store.

  • Semi-breaking. Changed the location of unscoped packages in the virtual global store. They will now be stor

issues.title: What are the differences between 'FactoeizePhys' and 'FactorizePhysBig'?
issues.body: First of all, I would like to express my gratitude to you and your team for your excellent work and for making the source code available to the community. I noticed that there are 'FactorizePhys' and 'FactorizePhysBig' in the 'model' folder. Are there any differences between them?

issues.title: Model Size
issues.body:
Dear author, thank you very much for your this work. 
I have a question about the Model Size on page 17. First of all, the Model Parameters I got are completely consistent with the data you presented in the paper. Taking PhysFormer as an example, our Model Parameters are both 7380871. Subsequently, I queried the data type of the code FP32, so the process of calculating the Model Size is 7380871*4/ (1024*1024) ≈ 28.15 MB. May I ask how the Model Size of PhysFormer (29.80MB) in your paper was calculated? Looking forward to your reply.

issues.title: Discrepancy in Results for iBVP_PhysNet on PURE Dataset
issues.body: Dear authors, I have been testing the model `iBVP_PhysNet.pth` using the configuration file `iBVP_PURE_PHYSNET_BASIC.yaml` on the full PURE dataset (58 folders). However, I observed that the results I obtained during testing significantly differ from the results presented in your paper, as highlighted in the red box in the table. Specifically, the metrics I obtained for the PURE dataset testing do not align with the reported values in the paper. Additionally, the signal extracted during the model evaluation on this dataset is as follows: Could you kindly clarify if there are any additional preprocessing steps, parameter adjustments, or dataset-specific considerations that need to be applied for testing? Your insights would be greatly appreciated. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Best regards, Alaa Hajr

pull_requests.title: Add README.md documentation for medical Llama 3.2-3B fine-tuning pipeline
pull_requests.body:
Created professional documentation for the medical textbook fine-tuning pipeline that processes raw medical content into QA pairs and trains Llama 3.2-3B.

## Documentation Structure

- **Project Overview**: Fine-tuning pipeline goal and approach
- **Workflow Pipeline**: 4-stage process from raw textbook data to inference
  - Data chunking by section headers → JSONL export
  - QA pair generation for instruction tuning
  - Model fine-tuning on generated dataset
  - Inference testing of fine-tuned model
- **Resources**: Links to HuggingFace dataset (`Rk_medical_QA`) and model (`medical-llama-3.2-3B`)
- **System & Data Info**: References to hardware configuration and dataset statistics files

The README provides clear navigation through the notebooks and external resources needed to understand and replicate the fine-tuning process.

issues.title: Phase 6: Progressive Escalation - Refactor Workflow Integration
issues.body:
## Phase 6: Refactor Workflow Integration (Week 6)

**Epic:** Progressive Tier Escalation System (v4.1.0)
**Dependencies:** Phase 5 issue

### Overview
Extend progressive escalation to refactor workflows with per-file granularity.

### Tasks
- [ ] Create `ProgressiveRefactorWorkflow`
- [ ] Implement per-file granularity
- [ ] Add refactor-specific failure detection
- [ ] Integration testing

### Refactor-Specific Features
- **Granularity**: Per-file (vs per-test for test-gen)
- **Quality Signals**:
  - Syntax errors (AST parsing)
  - Test pass rate (ensure refactor doesn't break tests)
  - Code complexity (lower is better)
  - Coverage maintenance

### Example Usage
```bash
# Refactor all Python files in src/
empathy workflow run refactor --progressive --target src/

# Refactor specific module
empathy workflow run refactor --progressive --target src/complex_module.py
```

### Deliverables
- [ ] ProgressiveRefactorWorkflow implemented
- [ ] Per-file escalation working
- [ ] Refactor-spe

**Epic:** Progressive Tier Escalation System (v4.1.0)

issues.title: Phase 4: Progressive Escalation - Cost Management & Approval
issues.body:
## Phase 4: Cost Management & Approval (Week 4)

**Epic:** Progressive Tier Escalation System (v4.1.0)
**Dependencies:** Phase 3 issue

### Overview
Implement comprehensive cost management including estimation, approval prompts, budgets, and tracking.

### Tasks
- [ ] Implement cost estimation logic
- [ ] Add approval prompts (CLI)
- [ ] Implement auto-approve-under budget
- [ ] Add cost tracking in telemetry
- [ ] Create cost analysis reports

### Cost Features
- **Upfront Estimation**: Show estimated cost before execution
- **Progressive Approval**: Prompt before each escalation
- **Auto-Approve Budget**: `--auto-approve-under $X.XX`
- **Budget Enforcement**: Warn or abort on exceeded budget
- **Cost Tracking**: Save cost breakdown per tier

### CLI Examples
```bash
# With approval prompts
empathy workflow run test-gen --progressive

# Auto-approve under $5
empathy workflow run test-gen --progressive --auto-approve-under 5.00

# Hard budget limit
empathy workflow run test-gen --progr

- [ ] Token counting using Anthropic SDK
- [ ] All rough estimates replaced
- [ ] Cost accuracy within 1% of actual bills

pull_requests.title: Release v5.0.0 - Agent Coordination & Dashboard
pull_requests.body:
## 🚀 Release v5.0.0 - Agent Coordination & Dashboard

This PR merges the Phase 2 Anthropic-native implementation, introducing comprehensive agent coordination patterns and a zero-dependency dashboard.

---

## ✨ New Features

### 6 Agent Coordination Patterns

**Pattern 1: Agent Heartbeat Tracking**
- Real-time agent status monitoring
- 30-second TTL with automatic expiration
- Progress tracking and task visibility

**Pattern 2: Coordination Signals**
- Inter-agent communication
- Permission enforcement (CONTRIBUTOR+ required)
- Signal types: ready, task_complete, checkpoint, error

**Pattern 3: Event Streaming** 
- Real-time event distribution
- Consumer group support
- Ordered event delivery

**Pattern 4: Real-time Event Streaming**
- Continuous event stream monitoring
- Dashboard integration

**Pattern 5: Approval Gates** ✋
- Human-in-the-loop for critical operations
- Approval/rejection workflow
- Timeout handling

**Pattern 6: Feedback Loop**

pull_requests.title: feat: ModelRouter integration for cost optimization
pull_requests.body:
## Summary
- Integrates ModelRouter into EmpathyLLM core for smart model selection
- Routes tasks to appropriate model tiers (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) based on complexity
- Includes ConversationSummaryIndex for session context and handoffs
- Adds `empathy sync-claude` CLI for CLAUDE.md auto-sync

## Cost Optimization Tiers
- **CHEAP** (Haiku): summarize, classify, triage → ~80-96% savings
- **CAPABLE** (Sonnet): generate_code, fix_bug → Default for most tasks  
- **PREMIUM** (Opus): coordinate, synthesize_results → Complex reasoning

## Test plan
- [x] All 72 routing tests passing
- [x] All core tests passing
- [x] Verify Preview deployment

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Add synthetic HealthKit sample data for demos/testing
issues.body:
### Problem

Contributors  often don’t have enough meaningful samples for testing or exploring the app. This leads to empty UI states and makes development slower.

### Solution

Provide an optional example or helper using synthetic HealthKit data. I built a small generator that creates realistic steps, workouts, heart-rate and sleep samples: https://github.com/aminbenarieb/healthkit-data-generator 

If helpful, I can tailor presets for HealthGPT and contribute a small integration example.

### Additional context

Working with HealthKit during development is often slow:
 - real data is limited
- syncing to the simulator is impossible
-  and charts look empty without meaningful samples
- exporting/importing data could be hard

 Synthetic data helps avoid these pitfalls and speeds up testing

### Solution

Migrate HealthKit data queries to SpeziHealthKit wrappers to simplify them and provide a good example of how to fully leverage SpeziHealthKit for this sort of application.

issues.title: Migrate to Swift Testing from XCTest
issues.body:
### Problem

With [Swift Testing](https://github.com/swiftlang/swift-testing) being added to the Swift 6 toolchain and the published images & Xcode already containing it, we should migrate all of our Swift repos towards the modern Swift Testing framework.

### Solution

All Swift unit tests and should migrate from `XCTest` to the new [Swift Testing framework](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/swift-testing/) included in the Swift 6 toolchain. We can follow the documentation article to [migrating a test from XCTest](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/testing/migratingfromxctest).

Contributes working on this issue should first watch the swift-testing WWDC videos:
- [Meet Swift Testing](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2024/10179)
- [Go further with Swift Testing](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2024/10195)

We should take advantage of [test suites](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/testing/organizingtests), [parameterized tests](https://developer.ap

issues.title: Chain LLM Queries to Identify Required HealthKit Data
issues.body:
### Problem

We currently package all the available HealthKit data to the LLM query for a user's request.

### Solution

1. Query the GPT API to ask what health data would be needed to answer the question (a fixed set of types that the user gave permissions to and the needed date range)
2. Query the HealthKit API (with the selected type and date range)
3. Pass the requested data including the question to the GPT API

### Code of Conduct

- [X] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct and Contributing Guidelines

issues.title: More Granular HealthKit Integration
issues.body:
### Use Case

It would be great to enable the support for more HealthKit types and access to the data from the current day.

### Problem

The current HealthKit data types used in responses are preselected in the code. It would be good to allow a user to select which HealthKit data they want to share and continuously update it while they are using the application.

### Solution

- [ ] Provide a UI to ask the user for a fine granular selection of HealthKit data.
- [ ] Allow a user to update the HealthKit access and remove the access to selected data types in the application in addition to limiting the access in the Health app. 

### Code of Conduct

- [X] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct and Contributing Guidelines

issues.title: Stream GPT Responses
issues.body:
### Use Case

It would be great if the UI and logic would support streaming GPT responses to the client UI.

### Problem

The streaming of responses to the client is currently broken and tracked in https://github.com/MacPaw/OpenAI/issues/14

### Solution

Incorporate the feature once https://github.com/MacPaw/OpenAI/issues/14 is closed.

# Adds support for on-device LLMs with SpeziLLMLocal

## :recycle: Current situation & Problem
Currently, the app can only use OpenAI models such as GPT-4. However, users may prefer to run an LLM on-device for increased privacy. This is now supported via the [SpeziLLMLocal](https://swiftpackageindex.com/stanfordspezi/spezillm/documentation/spezillmlocal) target of the [SpeziLLM module](https://github.com/Stanfordspezi/spezillm) and can be enabled in HealthGPT.

## :gear: Release Notes 
- Adds a step in onboarding for downloading and storing the Llama3 8B model
- Adds an `--llmLocal` feature flag for toggling local execution
- Adds an onboarding step allowing the user to choose between OpenAI or local execution
- Updates the `HealthDataInterpreter` to use the local LLM if the flag is set

## :books: Documentation
Updated documentation

## :white_check_mark: Testing
- Adds UI tests for local LLM configuration during onboarding

# Stream responses from model

## :recycle: Current situation & Problem
Currently we wait until receiving a complete response from the model in order to display it to the user. If the response takes a long time to generate, the user has to wait.

## :bulb: Proposed solution
This PR allows for streaming the response from the model as it is generated using an `AsyncThrowingStream` which is supported in the [MacPaw/OpenAI](https://github.com/MacPaw/OpenAI) package as of release 0.2.0.

pull_requests.body:
<!--

SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

-->

# Refactor for reusability

## :recycle: Current situation & Problem
Currently, all of the OpenAI querying logic is run inside `MainInputView`. We need to separate the UI and the logic in such a way that it can be easily updated, extended, and reused.

## :bulb: Proposed solution
- Creates a `MessageManager` passed as an EnvironmentObject that manages the state of queries and messages.
- Creates an `OpenAIManager` that manages interactions with OpenAI's API.
- Creates a `PromptGenerator` to assemble prompts in a modular fashion.
- Refactors health data fetching code to use Swift concurrency.
- Fixes error in sleep data calculation.

Thank you @PSchmiedmayer for your assistance with the above!

pull_requests.title: Update mypy-protobuf requirement from ^3.0.0 to >=3,<6
pull_requests.body:
Updates the requirements on [mypy-protobuf](https://github.com/nipunn1313/mypy-protobuf) to permit the latest version.
Changelog

Sourced from mypy-protobuf's changelog.

5.0.0

  • Use builtins.property to handle conflicts with fields named property
  • Mangle all non provided message type imports, this prevents conflicts with field names like collections, builtins, etc.
    • Do not mangle message imports, as that would be a breaking change.
  • BREAKING: Refactor import statements
    • Move to model similar to protoc pyi generator. This prevents name collisions in field names
      • from test.a.b import c as _c
      • If multiple names collied, append _{count}
        • from test.a.x import c as _c_1

pull_requests.title: [BUG-PROMPT][Updated prompts to stop patients & staff from predicting the future
pull_requests.body:
# HospitalSim: Enforce Non-Predictive Prompts For All Agents

## Description

This PR focuses solely on correcting agent prompting so that agents do not predict the future or speak for others. It strengthens the system prompts across all agents and sample patients to ensure responses are grounded only in information actually provided during the interaction.

## Scope of Changes

### In `hospital_sim/main.py`

1. Patient default system prompt
   - Added a CRITICAL CONVERSATION RULES section explicitly prohibiting:
     - Predicting or assuming what staff will say or do
     - Anticipating questions
     - Describing others' thoughts or actions
   - Added a RESPONSE PATTERN section instructing patients to reply only to what was actually said and to keep answers directly relevant.

2. Staff agent system prompts (executives, doctors, nurses, receptionist)
   - Replaced/augmented CONVERSATION GUIDELINES with CRITICAL CONVERSATION RULES mirroring the same non-prediction con

pull_requests.title: [BUGF-PROMPT][Improved realism by fixing prompts and adding Misdiagnosis and re-diagnosis]
pull_requests.body:
## Description

This PR significantly enhances the HospitalSim multi-agent hospital simulation system by fixing the core issue of agents predicting the future and behaving unrealistically. The main improvements are implemented in `hospital_sim/main.py` with additional updates to supporting files.

### Key Fixes in main.py:

1. **Medical Research System**
   - Added comprehensive medical knowledge base with symptom-to-condition mapping
   - Implemented evidence-based treatment options and risk factor analysis
   - Created realistic differential diagnosis generation
   - Added research notes and probability calculations

2. **Realistic Misdiagnosis System**
   - Implemented probability-based diagnostic errors (15-20% realistic error rate)
   - Added condition-specific accuracy rates (70-95% depending on condition)
   - Created common misdiagnosis patterns (e.g., heart attack vs. anxiety)
   - Added doctor experience factor affecting accuracy

3. **Enhanced Agent Behavio

pull_requests.body:
Updates the requirements on [ruff](https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff) to permit the latest version.
Release notes

Sourced from ruff's releases.

0.12.0

Release Notes

Check out the blog post for a migration guide and overview of the changes!

Breaking changes

  • Detection of more syntax errors

    Ruff now detects version-related syntax errors, such as the use of the match statement on Python versions before 3.10, and syntax errors emitted by CPython's compiler, such as irrefutable match patterns before the final case arm.

  • New default Python version handling for syntax errors

    Ruff will default to the latest supported Python version (3.13) when checking for the version-related

v1.10.2

💅 Cosmetic Output Improvements

In #250 and #258, @​facutuesca💰 added a nudge message with a magic link to pre-fill the creation of new Trusted Publishers configurations on PyPI. issues.title: 请问多轮对话的格式及样例，可以给个实例么
进行增量预训练一些问题
issues.body:
作者大大您好，我想对一个专业领域的数据集进行增量预训练（大约有40w个产品的描述），会增加通用领域的数据（专与通的比例1:7的样子），考虑用ChatGLM3-6b去做，想要学习到领域知识的同时尽可能去避免灾难性遗忘，有些问题想请教一下您：
1.模型是推荐用base还是chat来做增量预训练呢？
2.做完增量预训练后有必要拿通用领域的指令数据集再做一下SFT？
3.我们的显卡资源并不够，只有3块4090，同时数据量也不够，全参微调是做不了，用Lora会不会效果比较差，有没有比较好的建议或者方法？

期待您的回复，祝好！

issues.title: Questions Regarding Instructions #8 and #10
issues.body:
想请教一下作者，关于实践经验的两个问题，还望不吝赐教：
1. 第八条中“对于垂类模型，或许我们更应该关注PT的过程，而不是采集千万百万的SFT数据做训练，我们的建议是大规模预训练+小规模监督微调=超强的LLM模型；”，请问这里的“大规模预训练”是指继承通用大语言模型参数还是在垂类上预训练呢？“小规模监督微调”如何解决通用语言模型无法理解垂域专有词汇或背景知识不足的问题呢（目前是 RAG，但感觉效果还不够好）？如果以医疗chatbot 为例，这里的“千万百万的SFT”指的是人工标记的问答么？
2. 第十条中“预训练可以灌入知识，监督微调只是激活领域能力(无法关注知识)？预训练的知识与监督微调知识应该呼应？预训练几十GB的语料知识会被原来数万亿token预训练的模型知识淹没？”请问这里的“预训练几十GB的语料知识”中的“预训练”实际指的是二次预训练么，即在专用知识库上自回归训练么？“原来数万亿token预训练的模型知识”中的“预训练”实际指的是通用语言模型的预训练么？目前确实观察到了“预训练可以灌入知识”，即全参数的二次预训练可以引入新知识，但是通用语言能力会有损失。请教一下作者，对于有监督微调的数据量、数据选择及训练方式（全参数/LoRA-family）有没有什么经验性的指导意见。

感谢！

---

I would like to consult the author on two questions about practical experience, and I hope you could kindly provide your insights:
1. In the eighth article, it is stated that "For vertical models, perhaps we should pay more attention to the PT process, rather than collecting tens of millions of SFT data for training, our suggestion is large-scale pre-training + small-scale supervised fine-tuning = super strong LLM model." Could

issues.title: Addition: DoD‑based QA Dataset from Emergency War Surgery (US Public Domain)
issues.body:
I’ve released a U.S. public-domain medical QA dataset derived from *Emergency War Surgery* (DoD manual).

• 86 validated open‑ended QA pairs, chunked and formatted as JSONL  
• Designed for sovereign, offline AI, RAG pipelines  
• Fully licensed (CC0‑1.0, U.S. Government work)  
• Ideal for fine-tuning or benchmarking medical LLMs

🔗 Dataset: https://huggingface.co/datasets/Paxrad/EWS_v5_USONLY_final  
🔗 Dataset summary (GitHub Gist): https://gist.github.com/[YOUR_GIST_ID]

Please consider linking or integrating this in your resource listings or tooling.

issues.title: License for using the data
issues.body:
Hi Abachaa,

I don't see a common license as part of this github repo. Are you planning to add a license to this github to make it easier to use the datasets?

issues.title: Code for scraping answers
issues.body: Feel free to check this repo for scraping (most of) the answers: https://github.com/glicerico/medquad-scraper

issues.title: How can you actually crawl the answer for MedLineDrugs?
issues.body: I see that we only have questions pairs but there is no real method to crawl the answer from MedLineDrugs. Is it crawlable? Thank you so much!

issues.title: Any future plan to release the knowledge source of those question answer pairs?
issues.body:
Hi,

Thank you for releasing this amazing dataset which is a great contribution to ML in healthcare! I wonder if you have any future plan to release the knowledge source of all those question answer pairs as well to make it a great reading comprehension benchmark like SQuAD. I noticed that many questions can be answered using the webpages whose urls are mentioned in your dataset. I wonder if you are planning to clean up those webpages and extract relevant passages to support the reading comprehension task.
Thanks!

alok-devforge/MediScan.description: MediScan: AI-powered bone fracture detection system achieving 99.8% accuracy through deep learning. Features real-time X-ray analysis, transparent Grad-CAM visualizations, and clinical integration tools. Built with Python/FastAPI backend and responsive HTML/CSS frontend, making advanced medical diagnostics more accessible to healthcare providers.

pull_requests.title: Add Grad-CAM visualization styles and flip functionality
pull_requests.body:
This pull request introduces enhancements to the Grad-CAM visualization feature in `gradcam.html` and updates the `.vscode/settings.json` file to configure the Live Server port. The changes focus on improving the 3D flip animation, ensuring smooth transitions, and addressing rendering issues for Grad-CAM images.

### Grad-CAM Visualization Enhancements:
* **New CSS Classes for 3D Flip Animation**: Added `.visual-container`, `.visual-xray`, and `.visual-gradcam` classes to support 3D flip animations with smooth transitions and proper backface visibility handling. (`gradcam.html`, [gradcam.htmlR131-R179](diffhunk://#diff-366df2996033327872007a59c2c46b9ba100fdea695dddf0098e75dc67dc4699R131-R179))
* **Improved Flip Logic**: Refactored the flip button's event listener to toggle the `flipped` class on the container and ensure animations play correctly by forcing reflows. (`gradcam.html`, [gradcam.htmlL401-R458](diffhunk://#diff-366df2996033327872007a59c2c46b9ba100fdea695dddf0098e75dc67dc

pull_requests.title: improving the mobile responsiveness of the timeline view in gradcam.html
pull_requests.body:
This pull request focuses on improving the mobile responsiveness of the timeline view in `gradcam.html`, fixing image references, and simplifying 3D transformations. The most important changes include adjustments to CSS for better alignment and layout on mobile devices, corrections to image file paths, and the replacement of inline 3D transformation styles with reusable CSS classes.

### Mobile responsiveness improvements:
* Adjusted `.timeline-item` styles to fix alignment issues and ensure proper padding and positioning on mobile devices. Updated `.absolute` and other related classes for consistent layout. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-366df2996033327872007a59c2c46b9ba100fdea695dddf0098e75dc67dc4699L70-R73) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-366df2996033327872007a59c2c46b9ba100fdea695dddf0098e75dc67dc4699L82-R132)

### Image reference fixes:
* Corrected file paths for Grad-CAM and X-ray images to use the proper filenames (`wrist-gradcam.jpg` and `wrist-xray.jpg`).

### Simplification of 3D tran

pull_requests.title: improving the mobile responsiveness and visual alignment
pull_requests.body:
This pull request focuses on improving the mobile responsiveness and visual alignment of timeline elements in the `about.html` and `gradcam.html` files. The changes ensure better usability and aesthetics on smaller screens by repositioning timeline elements and adjusting styles for mobile devices.

### Mobile Responsiveness Improvements:

* **Repositioned Year Labels:** In `about.html`, year labels for timeline items were moved above the heading in mobile view and hidden in desktop view to improve readability.
* **Added Mobile-Specific Styles:** Introduced CSS rules in `gradcam.html` to adjust padding, alignment, and positioning of timeline elements for screens smaller than 768px. This includes fixes for text alignment, icon positioning, and column spacing.

### Visual Alignment Adjustments:

* **Adjusted Timeline Circle Positioning:** Updated the positioning of timeline circles in `gradcam.html` to align properly in mobile view, ensuring consistent placement across devices. [

pull_requests.title: Refactor navigation and team section styles; enhance mobile menu func…
pull_requests.body:
This pull request focuses on enhancing the user interface and user experience across multiple components of the application. Key improvements include modernizing the visual design of the footer, navigation, and detection results, as well as refining the functionality of the mobile navigation and header behavior. Additionally, new CSS styles were introduced to provide a polished and consistent look.

### UI/UX Enhancements

* **Detection Results (`detect-script.js`):**
  - Updated the "No fractures detected" message with improved typography and styling for better readability. 
  - Refactored the confidence level logic into a more concise ternary operator.
  - Enhanced the design of detection items with new classes for spacing, borders, shadows, and background color.

* **Footer Redesign (`footer-template.html`):**
  - Modernized the footer layout with a cleaner grid structure, updated typography, and hover effects for links.
  - Redesigned social media icons with hover animat

issues.title: feat: Add CSC dataset and update GSC dataset with RAG-HPO data
issues.body:
## Summary
Add CSC (Case Study Cohort) dataset and update GSC dataset by importing data from the RAG-HPO repository (https://github.com/PoseyPod/RAG-HPO). This involves converting datasets to PhenoBERT format and adding necessary metadata.

## Tasks

### Dataset Integration
- [ ] Fetch CSC and GSC datasets from https://github.com/PoseyPod/RAG-HPO

issues.title: feat(retrieval): Adaptive re-chunking for poor-quality retrieval results
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

Currently, Phentrieve performs text chunking once during preprocessing, then retrieves HPO terms for each chunk. When a chunk produces poor-quality retrieval results (e.g., low similarity scores, high variability, no hierarchically related terms), there's no mechanism to detect this and attempt adaptive re-chunking with different granularity.

**Scenario Example:**
```text
Input chunk: "Patient presents with kidney stones and frequent headaches"
```

This chunk combines two unrelated phenotypes. If the embedding model struggles to find a clear match (e.g., all results < 0.5 similarity with high variance), it may indicate that the chunk conflates multiple concepts and would benefit from sub-chunking.

## Current Behavior

1. Text is chunked once using selected strategy (`simple`, `semantic`, `detailed`, `sliding_window`)
2. Each chunk is embedded and queried against ChromaDB
3. Results are filtered by `MIN_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD` (default 0.3)
4. **Fallback exists on

issues.title: Production deployment shows 'development' environment in version info
issues.body:
## Problem

When deployed in production (e.g., https://phentrieve.kidney-genetics.org/), the version dialog shows **"Environment: development"** instead of "production".

## Screenshot Evidence

Version dialog shows:
- Frontend: 0.6.0 (Vue.js)
- API: 0.7.0 (FastAPI)
- CLI: 0.12.0 (Python)
- **Environment: development** ← Should be "production"

## Root Cause

In `api/version.py` line 43:
```python
"environment": os.getenv("ENV", os.getenv("ENVIRONMENT", "development")),
```

The environment variable `ENV` or `ENVIRONMENT` is not set in `docker-compose.yml`, so it defaults to "development".

## Solution

Add environment variable to `docker-compose.yml` in the `phentrieve_api` service:

```yaml
environment:
  - ENV=production
  # ... other env vars
```

Or add to `.env.docker.template`:
```bash
# Environment identifier
ENV=production
```

## Impact

- Misleading for users checking version info
- May affect logging/monitoring if environment-specific behavior is added later

🤖 Generated wi

## Problem

When deploying with a host volume mount (`/srv/phentrieve/data:/phentrieve_data_mount`), the embedded data bundle in the image is overridden by the host directory contents. If the data format changes between versions (e.g., adding `hpo_data.db`), the deployment fails because the host volume has stale data.

## Current Behavior

1. Docker build downloads and embeds the latest data bundle ✅
2. Host volume mount overrides embedded data at runtime
3. If host volume has old data structure → **startup fails**

```
[entrypoint] HPO database not found: /phentrieve_data_mount/hpo_data.db
[entrypoint] Data verification failed - exiting
```

## Expected Behavior

The entrypoint should detect missing/outdated data and either:
1. **Auto-sync**: Copy embedded data to volume if `manifest.json` is missing or outdated
2. **Clear error**: Provide instructions to update the volume data

## Proposed Solution

Add to `docker-entrypoint.sh`:

```bash
# Check if volume data needs sync from embedd

issues.title: feat(retrieval): Multi-vector HPO term embeddings with configurable aggregation
issues.body:
**Supersedes**: #33, #128

---

# Multi-Vector HPO Term Embeddings - Conceptual Design

## Key Decisions

- **Multi-vector is optional** - both index types coexist
- **Components**: label + individual synonyms + definition (single vector)
- **Benchmarking required** - compare single vs multi-vector retrieval quality
- **Aggregation caching** - cache results for repeated queries (minor optimization)

---

## Problem

Currently, each HPO term is embedded as **one vector** containing concatenated text:
```
Term: Seizure
Synonyms: Fits, Convulsions
Definition: A seizure is a transient occurrence...
```

This single-vector approach has limitations:
- Long definitions dilute the label/synonym signal
- Can't determine which component matched the query
- Query matching a synonym may score lower than optimal

## Solution: Multi-Vector Representation

Store **separate vectors** for each component:

```
HP:0001250 "Seizure":
  ├── label vector:      embed("Seizure")
  ├── synonym vectors:   embed

issues.title: fix: remove obsolete HPO terms from index
issues.body:
## Problem

Obsolete HPO terms are included in the vector index, causing incorrect matches.

**Example**: Query "Rod-cone dystrophy" returns `HP:0008036` ("obsolete Rod-cone dystrophy") instead of the current term.

## Impact

- 13.5% of HPO terms don't self-match in embedding tests
- Some "wrong" matches are actually obsolete→current term confusion
- Degrades retrieval accuracy

## Solution

Filter out obsolete terms during `phentrieve data prepare` / index building:
- Check for "obsolete" prefix in label
- Check `is_obsolete` flag in HPO JSON if available

## Files

- `phentrieve/data_processing/hpo_parser.py`
- `phentrieve/indexing/index_builder.py`

issues.title: feat(text-processing): Add flag to limit top N HPO terms per chunk in phenopacket output
issues.body:
## Feature Request

Add a flag to limit the number of HPO terms returned per chunk when generating phenopacket output.

## Use Case

When processing clinical text into phenopackets, users may want to control the number of HPO terms included per chunk to:
- Reduce noise from low-confidence matches
- Focus on most relevant phenotypes
- Control phenopacket size for downstream tools

## Proposed Solution

Add a `--top-n-per-chunk` flag to text processing commands:

```bash
phentrieve text process "clinical text" --top-n-per-chunk 5
```

This would:
- Keep only the top N highest-scoring HPO terms for each semantic chunk
- Apply after retrieval and re-ranking (if enabled)
- Affect phenopacket output format specifically

## Labels

enhancement, text-processing

## Goal

Document the current implementation and benchmark alternative strategies to inform future optimizations.

- Related to #33 (multi-vector representation research)

4. Benchmark current approach with metrics (recall, precision, MRR)
5. Compare with alternative strategies (lab

issues.title: Benchmark command fails when no model is specified

**Description:**
When running `phentrieve benchmark run` without the `--model-name` option, the command fails with an error about a missing Chroma collection for an empty model name. **Expected Behavior:**
The command should use the default model from the configuration and run successfully, as it does when `--model-name "FremyCompany/BioLORD-2023-M"` is explicitly provided.

**Root Cause:**
In `phentrieve/cli/benchmark_commands.py`, the line `model_name=model_name or "" ` always passes a string, even when `model_name`

issues.title: Negation particles and operators affect retrieval accuracy across languages
issues.body:
## Problem

Negation particles and operators affect HPO term retrieval differently than expected across all supported languages (EN, DE, ES, FR, NL):

- **German**: "Kein Erythem" (no erythema) vs "Erythem" (erythema) produce different retrieval results
- **English**: "No fever" vs "fever" may have similar issues
- Particles like "no", "kein", "nicht", "sin", "non", "geen", etc. may interfere with semantic matching
- Stop words and other operators might need special handling before embedding

## Examples

- German: "Kein Erythem" vs "Erythem"
- English: "No fever" vs "fever"
- Spanish: "Sin fiebre" vs "fiebre"
- French: "Pas de fièvre" vs "fièvre"
- Dutch: "Geen koorts" vs "koorts"

All should retrieve similar HPO terms, with assertion detection marking negated cases appropriately.

## Possible Solutions

1. Remove negation particles before embedding/retrieval?
2. Handle stop words and operators separately in preprocessing?
3. Normalize text to strip particles while preserving assertio

issues.title: test_embeddings_real.py fails on macOS - expects CPU but gets MPS device
issues.body:
## Description

Test `tests/unit/core/test_embeddings_real.py::TestLoadEmbeddingModel::test_default_model_cpu` fails on macOS because the embedding model is automatically moved to MPS (Metal Performance Shaders) device instead of CPU.

## Error

```
AssertionError: expected call not found.
Expected: to('cpu')
  Actual: to('mps')
```

## Root Cause

The test assumes the model will use CPU, but PyTorch automatically selects MPS on Apple Silicon Macs when available. The test mock verification is too strict.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. Update test to accept both `cpu` and `mps` as valid devices
2. Force CPU device in the test setup with `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=""`
3. Mock device selection to return CPU regardless of platform

## Environment

- macOS with Apple Silicon (MPS available)
- PyTorch with MPS support enabled

issues.title: feat: Pre-built data distribution system via GitHub Releases
issues.body:
## Problem Statement

Currently, every Phentrieve installation must rebuild HPO databases and vector indexes from scratch:

1. **`phentrieve data prepare`** - Downloads HPO JSON, parses it, builds SQLite database (~30s)
2. **`phentrieve index build`** - Downloads embedding model, embeds 19,534 HPO terms, builds ChromaDB index (~5-15 min)

This creates several problems:

| Issue | Impact |
|-------|--------|
| **Slow first-run** | 5-15 minutes before first query |
| **Redundant compute** | Every installation rebuilds identical data |
| **Docker builds slow** | Must rebuild index on every deploy |
| **Non-reproducible** | Different machines may produce slightly different indexes |
| **CI/CD flaky** | Network/compute variability affects tests |

## Proposed Solution

Create a **pre-built data distribution system** using **GitHub Releases** in a dedicated `phentrieve-data` repository.

### Architecture

```
phentrieve-data repository (GitHub Releases)
├── v2025.03.03-biolord-2023-m.tar.gz

issues.title: Fix: Frontend healthcheck should use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost
issues.body:
## Problem

The frontend healthcheck fix in commit 6dec0b5 (#105) uses `localhost` in the wget command, but Alpine-based nginx images don't have proper localhost resolution, causing the healthcheck to fail with "Connection refused".

**Current healthcheck:**
```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD wget -q --spider http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
```

**Error:**
```
wget: can't connect to remote host: Connection refused
```

**But nginx IS listening:**
```
Proto  Local Address   State   PID/Program
tcp    0.0.0.0:8080    LISTEN  1/nginx
```

**And `127.0.0.1` WORKS:**
```bash
docker exec container wget -O - http://127.0.0.1:8080/health
# Returns: OK
```

## Root Cause

Alpine Linux minimal images often don't have `/etc/hosts` entries or proper localhost resolution. The healthcheck should use `127.0.0.1` directly.

## Fix

Change healthcheck in `frontend/Dockerfile`:
```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --star

issues.title: Deploy: hpo_core_data needs write access for hpo_data.db generation
issues.body:
## Problem

The security-hardened `docker-compose.yml` mounts the data directory as read-only (`:ro`), but the HPO database file (`hpo_data.db`) needs to be created during data preparation, which requires write access.

```

**Root cause:**
```yaml
volumes:
  # Data mount - read-only by default for security

  # Only indexes has write access
  - ${PHENTRIEVE_HOST_DATA_DIR}/indexes:/phentrieve_data_mount/indexes:rw
```

The `hpo_core_data` subdirectory is inside the read-only mount and needs write access for:
- Creating `hpo_data.db` during `phentrieve data prepare`
- Potentially updating HPO data

## Impact

- Cannot run `phentrieve data prepare` inside the container
- Fresh deployments that need to generate the HPO database will fai

The recent Docker security hardening changes introduced several breaking changes that affect existing deployments. There is no migration guide or upgrade documentation, causing production failures when users update.

## Related Issues

This is a meta-issue tracking the deployment problems discovered during a production update:

| Issue | Problem | Severity |
|-------|---------|----------|
| #105 | Frontend healthcheck uses `curl` (not in Alpine) | Low - cosmetic |
| #106 | CORS `allowed_origins` missing production URL | High - blocks queries |
| #107 | HPO data format changed to SQLite | Critical - service unavailable |
| #108 | Non-root user can't access root-owned data | Critical - service unavailable |
| #109 | docker-compose.yml missing `env_file` directive | High - silent failures |

## Breaking Changes Introduced

### 1. Container User Change
- **Before:** Containers ran as root
- **After:** API runs as UID 10001, Frontend runs as UID 101
- **Impact:** Existing data o

issues.title: Deploy: docker-compose.yml missing env_file directive causes silent failures
issues.body:
## Problem

The `docker-compose.yml` references environment variables like `${PHENTRIEVE_HOST_DATA_DIR}` but does not include an `env_file:` directive. This requires users to manually pass `--env-file .env.docker` on every docker-compose command, which is easy to forget and causes silent failures.

**Observed behavior:**
```bash
$ docker-compose restart phentrieve_api
WARN: The "PHENTRIEVE_HOST_DATA_DIR" variable is not set. Defaulting to a blank string.
invalid spec: :/phentrieve_data_mount:ro: empty section between colons
```

**Root cause:**
docker-compose only reads `.env` by default, not `.env.docker`. The project uses `.env.docker` but docker-compose.yml doesn't specify this.

## Impact

- Volume mounts silently fail with empty paths
- Container starts but cannot access data
- API reports healthy but queries return 503
- Extremely difficult to debug without checking docker-compose warnings

## Current Configuration

**docker-compose.yml:**
```yaml
volumes:
  - ${PHENTRIEVE_HOST_D

issues.title: Deploy: ChromaDB permissions fail with non-root container user
issues.body:
## Problem

After updating to the security-hardened Docker configuration, the API fails to access the ChromaDB indexes due to permission mismatch between the container user and the host filesystem ownership.

**Error in logs:**
```
ERROR:root:Error connecting to ChromaDB: /phentrieve_data_mount/indexes: error returned from database: (code: 8) attempt to write a readonly database

ERROR:api.dependencies:API: Failed to init DenseRetriever for FremyCompany/BioLORD-2023-M.
```

## Root Cause

The new security-hardened `docker-compose.yml` runs the API as a non-root user:
```yaml
user: "10001:10001"
```

However, the existing data on the host was created by root:
```
/srv/phentrieve/data/indexes/
├── chroma.sqlite3  (owned by root:root)
└── subdirs/        (owned by root:root)
```

The container user (10001) cannot write to root-owned files.

## Impact

- API sta

issues.title: Frontend Docker healthcheck fails - curl not installed in Alpine nginx image
issues.body:
## Problem

The frontend Docker container's healthcheck fails because it uses `curl` which is not installed in the Alpine nginx image.

**Observed behavior:**
```
docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health}}' phentrieve_repo-phentrieve_frontend-1
```
Shows container as "unhealthy" with healthcheck output:
```
/bin/sh: curl: not found
```

**Root cause:**
The Dockerfile uses `nginx:alpine` as base image, which doesn't include curl by default. The healthcheck in `docker-compose.yml` or Dockerfile uses:
```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8080/"]
```

## Impact

- Container is marked as "unhealthy" even though nginx is running correctly
- Health monitoring tools report false negatives
- Docker orchestration (restart policies based on health) may behave unexpectedly

## Suggested Fix

**Option 1 (Recommended):** Use wget instead (already available in Alpine):
```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/"]
  interval:

issues.title: fix: API Docker build exceeds 30-minute timeout in GitHub Actions
issues.body:
## Problem
API Docker image build consistently times out at 30 minutes in GitHub Actions while Frontend builds complete in ~2 minutes.

## Root Cause
- Heavy ML dependencies (PyTorch, sentence-transformers, spaCy) require 20-30+ minutes to install
- `type=gha` cache has known reliability issues with large images ([docker/build-push-action#545](https://github.com/docker/build-push-action/issues/545))
- Cache export can timeout after 600 seconds

## Solution
1. Switch from `type=gha` to registry cache (`type=registry`) - more reliable for large images
2. Increase timeout from 30 to 60 minutes
3. Add workflow concurrency control to prevent overlapping builds
4. Fix Hadolint warnings (DL3003, DL3013)

## References
- [Docker Docs: Registry Cache](https://docs.docker.com/build/cache/backends/registry/)
- Analysis: `plan/01-active/DOCKER-BUILD-TIMEOUT-FIX.md`

issues.title: feat(frontend): Conversation persistence and virtual scrolling optimization
issues.body:
## Problem

Frontend chat interface has critical performance and UX issues:
- ❌ DOM bloat: `queryHistory` grows indefinitely, degrading performance with 100+ queries
- ❌ State loss: All conversation lost on navigation or page reload
- ❌ No reset: Users cannot clear conversation and start fresh
- ❌ No user control: Cannot configure conversation retention

## Solution

Centralize state in Pinia with persistence and implement virtual scrolling:

1. **Pinia Store**: `useConversationStore` with `pinia-plugin-persistedstate` v4 (already installed)
2. **Virtual Scrolling**: `vue-virtual-scroller` to render only visible items
3. **User Settings**: History length slider, storage stats, clear button
4. **Logo Reset**: Click logo to reset application state (with v-dialog confirmation)

**Tech Debt Cleanup**: Refactor `disclaimer.js` to use persist plugin (currently manual localStorage)

## Timeline

**2-3 days** (Big Bang migration recommended)

- Phase 1: Pinia conversation store with v4 persist

issues.title: feat(benchmark): Enhance single-term HPO retrieval benchmark with MTEB-aligned metrics
issues.body:
## Problem

Current single-term retrieval benchmark has limitations for rigorous embedding model comparison:

- ❌ Missing industry-standard metrics (NDCG@K, Recall@K, Precision@K)
- ❌ No statistical significance testing (can't determine if Model A > Model B)
- ❌ Small test dataset (9 cases in `tiny_v1.json` - insufficient statistical power)
- ❌ No difficulty stratification (all test cases treated equally)
- ❌ Language isolation not explicit (risk of mixing evaluation contexts)

**Current metrics:** MRR, HR@K, MaxOntSim
**Missing:** NDCG@K (MTEB/BEIR primary metric), Recall@K, bootstrap confidence intervals

## Solution

Align with MTEB/BEIR standards while preserving HPO-specific metrics:

### 1. Add Industry-Standard Metrics

| Metric | Purpose | Priority |
|--------|---------|----------|
| **NDCG@K** | Rank-aware relevance (MTEB primary) | High |
| **Recall@K** | Proportion of relevant items found | High |
| **Precision@K** | Retrieval efficiency | Medium |
| **MAP@K** | Average prec

issues.title: feat: unified output format with Phenopackets integration
issues.body:
## Problem

Current output formats are fragmented:
- ❌ Query and text processing use incompatible schemas
- ❌ No clinical interoperability (GA4GH standards)
- ❌ Lost provenance (can't trace HPO terms to source text)
- ❌ Cannot save/load analysis state

## Solution

Unified three-layer architecture with GA4GH Phenopackets v2.0:

1. **Internal**: `PhentrieveAnalysis` wrapper with embedded Phenopacket
2. **Converters**: Pluggable exporters (JSON, Phenopacket, CSV)
3. **Export**: User-selectable formats

**Key Design** (per code review):
- Use computed properties (eliminate duplication)
- Start with 3 formats (JSON, Phenopacket, CSV)
- One-way Phenopacket conversion (lossy by design)
- Defer interactive mode to Phase 3

## Timeline

**5 weeks total** (3 MVP + 2 polish)

### Phase 1: MVP (3 weeks)
- Week 1: Core data structures with Pydantic validation
- Week 2: JSON/Phenopacket/CSV exporters
- Week 3: CLI integration (\`--output-format\`, \`--output-file\`)

### Phase 2: Polish (2 weeks)
-

issues.title: Research: Advanced negation detection improvements
issues.body:
## Context

Following implementation of ConText algorithm for multilingual negation detection (PR #82, fixes #79), this tracks potential enhancements for more sophisticated clinical negation handling.

**Current State**: ConText-based direction-aware negation detection with TERMINATE/PSEUDO handling (F1 ~85-90% typical).

## Proposed Improvements

### 1. Additional ConText Categories (Medium Priority)
**What**: Implement remaining ConText assertion categories beyond NEGATED_EXISTENCE
- `POSSIBLE_EXISTENCE` - "possible", "might have"
- `HISTORICAL` - "history of", "previous"
- `HYPOTHETICAL` - "if patient develops", "risk of"
- `FAMILY` - "mother has", "family history of"

**Why**: Distinguishes uncertainty/temporality from simple negation
**Effort**: Low (rules exist in ConText standard)
**When**: When users report misclassification of uncertain/historical findings

### 2. Cross-Sentence Scope Detection (Low Priority)
**What**: Extend negation scope beyond sentence boundaries
**Example

- Add MCP (Model Context Protocol) server support for AI assistant integration
- Expose 3 MCP tools: `query_hpo_terms`, `process_clinical_text`, `calculate_term_similarity`
- Support both stdio transport (Claude Desktop) and HTTP transport (same-domain `/mcp` endpoint)
- Enable MCP HTTP in Docker via `ENABLE_MCP_HTTP=true` (default in production)

## Changes
- **New MCP module** (`api/mcp/`): config, server factory, HTTP server entry points
- **CLI commands**: `phentrieve mcp serve`, `phentrieve mcp serve --http`, `phentrieve mcp info`
- **Router updates**: Added `operation_id` to query, text processing, and similarity endpoints
- **Docker support**: `ENABLE_MCP_HTTP` env var, `fastapi-mcp` in Dockerfile, entrypoint permission fix
- **Makefile targets**: `mcp-serve`, `mcp-serve-http`, `mcp-info`, `mcp-install`
- **Documentation**: `docs/mcp-server.md` with deployment guide and troubleshooting

## Usage

{

pull_requests.title: feat: Add multi-vector indexing and retrieval support
pull_requests.body:
## Summary
Implements multi-vector representation for HPO terms, enabling separate embeddings for labels, definitions, and synonyms with intelligent aggregation during retrieval.

## Key Features
- Enhanced `build_chromadb_index` to support single and multi-vector index types
- Updated `chromadb_orchestrator` to orchestrate multi-vector index creation
- Introduced `multi_vector_document_creator` for generating documents with separate vectors for labels, synonyms, and definitions
- Implemented 7 aggregation strategies in `aggregation.py` for combining multi-vector scores:
  - `label_only` - Use only label score
  - `label_synonyms_max` - Maximum of label or synonyms (default)
  - `label_synonyms_min` - Minimum (conservative)

pull_requests.title: feat(benchmark): add new evaluation metrics and statistical analysis utilities

The main focus is on expanding the set of supported metrics to align with industry standards (MTEB/BEIR), improving reporting in documentation, and updating the evaluation pipeline to compute, aggregate, and report these new metrics for both dense and reranked retrieval. These changes make the evaluation more robust and nuanced, allowing for better comparison and analysis of model performance.

**Metrics Expansion and Calculation:**

pull_requests.title: fix(security): Remove vulnerable setuptools from base image
pull_requests.body:
## Summary

Removes vulnerable setuptools from the Python base image's system site-packages to resolve Trivy security alerts.

**Root Cause**: The base `python:3.11-slim-bookworm` image ships with setuptools 65.5.1 in `/usr/local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/`. Even though we upgrade setuptools to >=78.1.1 in the virtual environment, Trivy still detects the vulnerable system-level package.

**Solution**: Remove setuptools, _distutils_hack, and pkg_resources from the base image in the runtime stage. The application uses a clean virtual environment with upgraded setuptools, so system setuptools is not needed.

## Security Fixes

- **CVE-2025-47273** (CRITICAL): Path traversal/arbitrary file write in setuptools <78.1.1
- **CVE-2024-6345** (HIGH): Path traversal in package URL in setuptools <70.0.0

## Test Plan

- [ ] Docker image builds successfully
- [ ] Trivy scan shows no setuptools CVEs
- [ ] Application functions correctly with virtual environment setuptools
- [ ] Health check endpo

## Summary

- **Cross-lingual Only**: Removed monolingual mode entirely (requires non-existent HPO translations)

### API & Configuration

pull_requests.title: fix(docker): Resolve API image build timeout in GitHub Actions
pull_requests.body:
## Summary
- Switch from `type=gha` to `type=registry` cache for Docker builds
- Increase API build timeout from 30 to 60 minutes  
- Add workflow concurrency control to prevent overlapping builds
- Fix Hadolint DL3003 warning

## Problem
API Docker build consistently exceeded 30-minute timeout due to:
- Heavy ML dependencies (PyTorch ~2GB, sentence-transformers, spaCy)
- `type=gha` cache reliability issues with large images ([docker/build-push-action#545](https://github.com/docker/build-push-action/issues/545))

## Why registry cache?
Registry cache is more reliable than `gha` for large ML images:
- No 10GB cache limit (uses container registry storage)
- `mode=max` caches ALL intermediate layers
- No cache export timeout issues
- [Docker Docs: Registry Cache](https://docs.docker.com/build/cache/backends/registry/)

## Test plan
- [ ] CI passes (Hadolint, workflow syntax)
- [ ] First build completes within 60 minutes
- [ ] Subsequent builds show cache hits in logs
- [ ] Build time < 15

## Changes

- **Added 4 safe dictionary access helpers** (`safe_get_nested`, `safe_get_list`, `log_missing_field`, `log_parsing_summary`)
- **Refactored `_extract_term_data_for_db()`** - safe metadata extraction with statistics tracking
- **Refactored `_parse_hpo_json_to_graphs()`** - graceful handling of missing fields and edge variants
- **Zero breaking changes** - all function signatures unchanged

### Testing

pull_requests.body:
## Summary

Implements the **ConText algorithm** (from medspaCy) for direction-aware clinical negation detection with TERMINATE scope boundaries and PSEUDO false positive prevention. Adds comprehensive multilingual support with 122 ConText rules across 5 languages.

**Resolves:** #79 (Missing German negation terms)

## Key Features

### 🎯 ConText Algorithm Implementation

- **Direction-Aware Detection:**
  - FORWARD: "No fever" → negates text AFTER trigger
  - BACKWARD: "Fever ruled out" → negates text BEFORE trigger  
  - BIDIRECTIONAL: French "ne...pas" → negates both sides

- **TERMINATE Scope Boundaries:**
  ```
  "No fever but has cough"
      ↓     ↓
   negated  affirmed (scope stops at "but")
  ```
  Prevents negation leakage across conjunctions

- **PSEUDO False Positive Prevention:**
  ```
  "Not only fever" → "not only" is PSEUDO, prevents false negation
  ```
  Tracks text spans to prevent shorter overlapping triggers

### 🌍 Multilingual Support (122 Rules)

| Language | Rul

pull_requests.title: perf(frontend): Vuetify tree-shaking + remove Vue devtools (-1.6 MB)
pull_requests.body:
## 🎯 Summary

Major frontend performance optimization reducing JavaScript bundle size by **73%** (2.3 MB → 624 KiB) through Vuetify tree-shaking and production devtools removal.

**Key Results**:
- Vuetify bundle: 798 KiB → 64 KiB (-92% reduction)
- Total JavaScript: 2,273 KiB → 624 KiB (-73% reduction)
- Expected Lighthouse score: 56 → 75-80 (Phase 1 of 8)

## 📦 Changes

### Performance Optimizations

1. **Vuetify Tree-Shaking** (-734 KiB)
   - Created modular plugin (`frontend/src/plugins/vuetify.js`)
   - Explicit imports: 41 components (vs all 100+)
   - 2 directives: Ripple, Tooltip

2. **Remove Production Devtools** (-62 KiB)
   - Added `__VUE_PROD_DEVTOOLS__: false` to vite.config.js
   - Tree-shakes @vue/devtools-kit from production builds

### Architecture Improvements

**DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)**:
- Single source of truth for Vuetify configuration
- No component imports scattered across files

**KISS (Keep It Simple)**:
- Simple explicit imports, no magic auto-registratio

pull_requests.title: refactor: Replace global HPO graph cache with thread-safe @lru_cache pattern
pull_requests.body:
## Summary

Refactored HPO graph data loading from mutable global state to thread-safe `@lru_cache` pattern, addressing anti-pattern while maintaining backward compatibility and improving testability.

## Problem Statement

The previous implementation in `phentrieve/evaluation/metrics.py` used module-level global variables:
```python
_hpo_ancestors: dict[str, set[str]] | None = None
_hpo_term_depths: dict[str, int] | None = None
```

**Issues:**
- ❌ **Not thread-safe** - Race conditions in FastAPI concurrent requests
- ❌ **Hard to test** - Mutable global state causes test pollution
- ❌ **Hidden dependencies** - Functions implicitly depend on global state
- ❌ **Inconsistent pattern** - Other parts use `@lru_cache` (utils.py, api/routers)

## Solution

Replaced with **wrapper pattern + `@lru_cache`**:

### Core Implementation

```python
@lru_cache(maxsize=1)
def _load_hpo_graph_data_impl(db_path: str) -> tuple[dict, dict]:
    """Internal cached implementation with normalized path."""

pull_requests.title: Refactor HPO data storage from files to SQLite database

pull_requests.title: feat(embeddings): Implement thread-safe model caching to prevent double loading
pull_requests.body:
## Summary

Resolves double loading issue where SentenceTransformer models were loaded multiple times into memory when used for both semantic chunking and HPO retrieval.

**Problem Solved:**
- **Before**: BioLORD-2023-M loaded twice (~1.3GB memory)
- **After**: Loaded once with caching (~650MB memory)  
- **Reduction**: 50% memory savings

## Key Changes

### Core Implementation
- **phentrieve/embeddings.py**: Thread-safe model registry with double-check locking pattern
  - Add `_MODEL_REGISTRY` dict caching models by name
  - Add `force_reload` parameter to bypass cache when needed
  - Add `clear_model_registry()` and `get_cached_models()` utilities
  - Support smart device switching (CUDA/MPS/CPU)
  - Coverage: 80% → 93%

- **phentrieve/cli/text_commands.py**: CLI integration to prevent double loading
  - Detect when same model used for chunking and retrieval
  - Reuse cached model instances
  - Remove unnecessary `trust_remote_code` parameters (handled automatically)

pull_requests.title: feat(frontend): Implement automatic log rotation with configurable limits
pull_requests.body:
## Summary

Implements automatic log rotation system to prevent performance degradation from unbounded log accumulation. The frontend logging system now uses a circular buffer (FIFO) pattern with configurable limits, statistics tracking, and persistent configuration.

**Key Features:**
- ✅ Automatic log rotation when exceeding configurable limit (default: 100 dev, 50 prod)
- ✅ Real-time statistics: received/dropped logs, memory usage monitoring
- ✅ Configurable UI with slider (10-500 range)
- ✅ localStorage persistence for user preferences
- ✅ Environment-aware defaults
- ✅ Full i18n support (EN, DE, ES, FR, NL)
- ✅ Comprehensive test coverage (29 tests, 100% passing)

**Performance Impact:**
- Prevents RAM bloat from indefinite log accumulation
- Reduces CPU overhead for large log arrays
- Maintains constant memory footprint

**Architecture:**
- **logConfig.js**: Centralized configuration following DRY principle
- **log.js store**: Circular buffer implementation with automatic rotatio

pull_requests.title: FDA Drug Chunker: Pharmaceutical Data Processing with Token Optimization
pull_requests.body:
## Summary
Implement specialized chunking pipeline for OpenFDA drug label data with token optimization and section-aware processing.

## Features
- **Token-aware chunking**: Precise token counting using tiktoken for OpenAI compatibility
- **Section-based processing**: Intelligent splitting by drug information sections (basic_info, usage_instructions, safety_info)
- **Batch processing**: Memory-efficient handling of large OpenFDA datasets
- **Comprehensive metadata**: Rich drug metadata including brand/generic names, manufacturers, warnings
- **Safety validation**: Token limit verification and compliance checking

## Chunking Strategies
- **Single-chunk**: For drug labels under token limits
- **Section-combination**: For large labels, combining related sections logically
- **Priority-based**: Critical information (warnings, dosage) prioritized in early chunks

## Technical Implementation

- **Paragraph-based**: For large content with context preservation

issues.title: MCP Stack your pipeline orchestrator for MCPs!
issues.body:
Hi mate!

I like the MCP initiative! The following information on [MCPStack](https://github.com/MCP-Pipeline/MCPStack) might be of interest.  In a nutshell, if you are familiar with [Scikit-learn,](https://scikit-learn.org/) MCPStack will let you build a successful chain of MCPs that resembles a Scikit-learn pipeline (instead of `preprocessor`, `transformers`, and `estimators`; you have `MCPs`); You can then execute this into any output, like `Claude Desktop`, or any other possibility (STDIO via `FastMCP`, etc.).

For the time being, we have two MCPs available ([see marketplace](https://mcpstack.readthedocs.io/en/latest/mcp_marketplace/)) mainly because they were investigated prior to being mirrored in [MCP-Pipeline](https://github.com/MCP-Pipeline) _(the org)_ by a team during one of their projects & papers at MIT, but more will undoubtedly be developed. To stack it with other MCPs with a few lines of code (`with_tool(<your_tool_instance>)`) or a few CLIs, maybe it would be of interes

biocontext-ai/registry.description: The BioContextAI Registry for biomedical MCP servers

issues.title: Alignment with the current effort to establish a standard MCP registry?
issues.body:
There is a current effort to establish a standard MCP registry: https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry with mechanisms for subregistries (https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/blob/main/docs/design/ecosystem-vision.md)

It enables to describe how the MCP servers can be configured and deployed, such as specifying the env variables available for a specific command, or the HTTP headers for streamable HTTP endpoints (e.g. Authorization typically), alongside with hints, defaults and descriptions that helps users configure the MCP servers

This enables to preconfigure a server through the registry frontend in a web form before installing it

I have myself created a small frontend on top of the official registry API to easily search, configure and install the MCP servers at https://vemonet.github.io/mcp-registry (there is no official UI yet, only the API for the registry). It helps to see what the MCP registry proposed format enables to do in term of MCP server configurat

issues.title: Submitting a new MCP server displays validation error `url: Must be a valid URL`
issues.body:
I am attempting to submit a new MCP server though the page at https://biocontext.ai/registry/editor
A validation error is raised: `url: Must be a valid URL`
It's unclear what the exact validation issue is, though. I think it's being raised for the `url` field in `maintainer`, and my provided value for that is `https://github.com/caufieldjh`.
An ORCID URL doesn't validate either (like `https://orcid.org/my-orcid?orcid=0000-0001-5705-7831`)
This field is also labeled Optional but submitting values without any for this field appears to raise the same validation error.

pull_requests.title: [WIP] Fix handling of MCP protocol errors in CI check
pull_requests.body:
Thanks for asking me to work on this. I will get started on it and keep this PR's description up to date as I form a plan and make progress.


<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> When checking remote MCP servers in the CI, we sometimes get:
> 
> saezlab/omnipath-next: Client error '406 Not Acceptable' for url 
> 'https://explore.omnipathdb.org/api/mcp'
> For more information check: 
> https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status/406
> Error: Process completed with exit code 1.
> 
> Server responses:
> {"jsonrpc":"2.0","error":{"code":-32000,"message":"Method not allowed."},"id":null}
> 
> This is correct behavior, as this uses the MCP protocol and thus simple HTTP GET requests can result in an error. In such cases, even though the response is 406, try to parse whether it is a jsonrpc response and accept it, if it is.


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

Name: JustChatChemistry

Description: This is a chemistry focused fork of just-chat by Longevity Genie, that adds domain specific tools for chemistry work. It provides an AI chat interface designed to handle chemistry related queries, molecular structure data, and conversions between chemical identifiers such as SMILES strings and IUPAC names.

The core purpose is to integrate chemistry reasoning into the chat system. It can convert between SMILES and IUPAC names using PubChem, identify functional groups in molecules, retrieve chemical properties such as molecular weight, perform 3D similarity searches for related compounds, and reason about chemistry topics using Large Language Models such as GPT, Claude, Llama 3.3, Deepseek, Mistral, and others.

Description: The evo2-mcp server exposes Evo 2 as a MCP server, providing tools for genomic sequence analysis. Any MCP-compatible client can use these tools to score, embed, and generate DNA sequences.

### Sequence Scoring

Evaluate DNA sequence likelihood under the Evo 2 model for genome quality assessment or variant comparison.

### Sequence Embedding

Extract learned feature representations from intermediate model layers for downstream analysis, similarity search, or machine learning pipelines.

### Sequence Generation

Generate novel DNA sequences conditioned on a prompt for synthetic biology, sequence design, or data augmentation.

### SNP Variant Effect Prediction

Score the effect of single-nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) on sequence likelihood for variant prioritization and interpretation.

Hi @eltzanis 🤗

I'm Niels and work as part of the open-source team at Hugging Face. I discovered your work on Arxiv and was wondering whether you would like to submit it to [hf.co/papers](https://hf.co/papers) to improve its discoverability.If you are one of the authors, you can submit it at https://huggingface.co/papers/submit.

The paper page lets people discuss about your paper and lets them find artifacts about it (your dataset for instance),
you can also claim the paper as yours which will show up on your public profile at HF, add Github and project page URLs.

I see that your `mAIstro` framework includes "Experiment data" as part of the workspace for reproducible runs. Would you like to host this dataset on https://huggingface.co/datasets?
Hosting on Hugging Face will give you more visibility/enable better discoverability, and will also allow people to do:

```python
from datasets import load_dataset

dataset = load_dataset("your-hf-org-or-username/your-dataset")
```

issues.title: Improve PDF Detailed Analysis section with proper markdown rendering
issues.body:
## Summary

The "Detailed Analysis" section in generated PDF reports currently strips all markdown formatting and renders as plain text, losing important structure and readability.

## Current Behavior

The `generatePDF()` function in `backend/src/services/report.ts` uses `stripMarkdown()` which removes all formatting:

```
doc.fontSize(10).fillColor('#2D343F').text(stripMarkdown(report.fullReport), { align: 'justify' });
```

This results in:
- `## Headers` becoming plain text (no larger font/bold)
- Paragraph breaks getting lost
- Numbered/bullet lists getting flattened into run-on text
- **Bold text** losing emphasis
- Citations blending into regular text
- Overall poor readability of complex medical analysis

## Expected Behavior

The Detailed Analysis section should properly render markdown elements:
- `## Section Headers` → Larger, bold headings
- Paragraph breaks preserved
- Numbered lists with proper indentation
- Bullet lists with proper indentation
- **Bold** and *italic* tex

Implement comprehensive observability including centralized logging, metrics collection, alerting, and distributed tracing.

    awsRegion: config.aws.region,

issues.title: 🎨 Frontend Enhancements
issues.body:
## Summary
Enhance the frontend with authentication UI, report history, error boundaries, and accessibility improvements to provide a complete user experience.

## Background
The current frontend has basic functionality but lacks:
- Login/registration pages (required after auth implementation)
- Report history view (users cannot see past analyses)
- Error boundaries (crashes show white screen)
- Accessibility compliance

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Login page with email/password form
- [ ] Registration page with validation
- [ ] Password reset flow pages
- [ ] Report history page showing past analyses
- [ ] Error boundary catches rendering errors gracefully
- [ ] Accessibility audit passes (WCAG 2.1 AA)

issues.title: ☁️ Production Infrastructure (IaC)
issues.body:
## Summary
Create Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform or AWS CDK to provision and manage production AWS resources consistently and repeatably.

## Background
Currently only LocalStack exists for local development. Production deployment requires:
- Real AWS S3 bucket with encryption and versioning
- DynamoDB tables with proper capacity
- Bedrock access with BAA for HIPAA compliance
- VPC and security groups
- CloudFront CDN for frontend

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Terraform/CDK scripts provision all required resources
- [ ] S3 bucket with versioning, encryption, and lifecycle policies
- [ ] DynamoDB tables with on-demand billing
- [ ] VPC with private subnets for backend
- [ ] Security groups properly configured
- [ ] CloudFront distribution for frontend
- [ ] AWS Bedrock BAA process documented
- [ ] Production environment files created
- [ ] Dockerfiles for containerized deployment
- [ ] Infrastructure can be destroyed and recreated reliably

## Technical Implementation

###

issues.title: 🔒 Security Hardening
issues.body:
## Summary
Harden the application security by removing development defaults, implementing proper secrets management, adding input validation, rate limiting, and request sanitization.

## Background
The current codebase contains **critical security vulnerabilities**:
- Hard-coded development secrets in `utils/config.ts`
- No input validation on API endpoints
- No rate limiting (vulnerable to DoS)
- No request sanitization

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All hard-coded secrets removed from codebase
- [ ] AWS Secrets Manager integration for production
- [ ] All API inputs validated with Joi
- [ ] Rate limiting implemented on all endpoints
- [ ] Request body/params sanitized
- [ ] Security headers properly configured
- [ ] No secrets in git history
- [ ] Security scan passes (Dependabot/Snyk)

## Technical Implementation

### 1. Remove Hard-coded Secrets
Current vulnerable code (`backend/src/utils/config.ts`):
```typescript
// REMOVE THESE DEFAULTS:
jwtSecret: process.env.JWT_SECRET || 'chan

issues.title: 🚀 CI/CD Pipeline Setup
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement automated CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions for continuous integration, testing, code quality scanning, and deployment automation.

## Background
Currently there is **no automation**. Per user requirements, the project must include:
- GitHub Actions workflows
- SonarQube Cloud integration
- Dependabot security scanning

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PR checks run automatically (lint, type-check, test)
- [ ] All tests must pass before merge
- [ ] SonarQube quality gate must pass
- [ ] Dependabot monitors for security vulnerabilities
- [ ] Staging deployment triggers on `develop` branch merge
- [ ] Production deployment triggers on `main` branch release
- [ ] Build artifacts are cached for performance
- [ ] Secrets are properly managed via GitHub Secrets

## Technical Implementation

Triggers on: PR to `main` or `develop`

issues.title: 🧪 Comprehensive Testing Suite
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement a complete testing strategy including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests to ensure code reliability and enable safe deployments.

## Background
Jest is configured in `package.json` but **zero tests exist**. The codebase has no test coverage, making refactoring risky and regression detection impossible. Per user requirements, code must pass for production deployment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit tests for all backend services (>80% coverage)
- [ ] API integration tests for all endpoints
- [ ] Frontend component tests for critical paths
- [ ] E2E tests for primary user flows
- [ ] Test data fixtures created
- [ ] Code coverage reporting configured
- [ ] Coverage thresholds enforced in CI (>80%)
- [ ] Tests pass in CI pipeline

- `storage.test.ts` - Mock S3 operations, test PDF extraction
- `report.

issues.title: 🔐 Authentication & Authorization Implementation
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement a complete authentication and authorization system using AWS Cognito to replace the current hard-coded `test-user` placeholder throughout the application.

## Background
The current codebase has **no authentication**. All API endpoints use a hard-coded `test-user` string (see `backend/src/handlers/analysis.ts` line 41). This is a critical security gap that must be resolved before production deployment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AWS Cognito User Pool is configured and deployed
- [ ] JWT validation middleware protects all `/api/*` endpoints
- [ ] Users can register with email/password
- [ ] Users can log in and receive valid JWT tokens
- [ ] Users can reset forgotten passwords
- [ ] Frontend routes requiring auth redirect to login
- [ ] User sessions expire appropriately (configurable TTL)
- [ ] All `test-user` TODO comments are resolved
- [ ] Unit tests cover auth middleware (>80% coverage)

## Technical Implementation

### Backend Tasks
1. **Cognito User Pool S

issues.title: HealthWeave Production Readiness Assessment & Plan
issues.body:
## Summary

HealthWeave has a **solid MVP foundation** with working document upload, AI analysis (via AWS Bedrock/Ollama/Anthropic), report storage, and PDF generation. The architecture is well-designed for HIPAA compliance. However, **significant work remains** to meet production deployment standards, particularly around authentication, testing, CI/CD, and security hardening.

---

## Current State: What's Complete ✅

### Frontend (Next.js 15 + React 19 + TypeScript)
- ✅ File upload with drag-and-drop (`react-dropzone`)
- ✅ Patient context input
- ✅ Analysis results display with markdown rendering
- ✅ PDF report download
- ✅ Responsive UI with Tailwind CSS
- ✅ Error handling and loading states

### Backend (Node.js + Express + TypeScript)
- ✅ Multi-file document upload (up to 25 files, 10MB each)
- ✅ S3 storage with server-side encryption
- ✅ PDF text extraction via `pdfjs-dist`
- ✅ AWS Bedrock integration with Claude
- ✅ Anthropic API direct fallback
- ✅ Ollama local LLM fallback
- ✅

pull_requests.title: fix sonarqube issues in this file.
pull_requests.body:
## All SonarQube issues in `bedrock.ts` are fixed:

1. **Readonly members** (lines 12-13): Marked `client` and `anthropicClient` as `readonly`.

2. **Number.parseFloat** (4 instances): Replaced `parseFloat` with `Number.parseFloat` in the logging helper.

3. **Optional chaining** (lines 101-102): Replaced `error.message && error.message.includes(...)` with `error.message?.includes(...)`.

4. **Unnecessary type assertion** (line 218): Removed the `as 'user' | 'assistant'` assertion; TypeScript infers the type.

5. **Cognitive complexity** (line 45): Reduced by extracting three helper methods:
   - `isBedrockUnavailable()` - checks if Bedrock is unavailable
   - `logAnalysisCompletion()` - logs completion metrics
   - `handleBedrockFallback()` - handles fallback logic

The code compiles successfully. The `analyzeHealthData` function is simpler, and the extracted helpers improve maintainability.
